transcript: I was in Osaka for three days and these were my favorite activities that I definitely recommend adding to your list. If you grew up eating cup noodles like me, then you have to visit the Cup Noodles Museum which is about a 30 minute train ride from the main city center. Cup noodles were actually invented in Osaka so it was really fun to visit the factory. Admission is free. You just pay for the cup noodles which are 500 yen each and they make such a great souvenir. You can decorate your cups before choosing. Three toppings. There were so many options and you can create some unique combinations. There's also a little museum and you gotta take photos in the Instant Ramen Tunnel. You don't need reservations for the Cup Noodles Factory, but if you want to do the Chicken Ramen experience where you make your own noodles from scratch, you'll need to book that in advance. And fair warning, those sell out super fast online hands down. The most unique activity we did was visit Mitera Kaiken which is an old multi tenant building where There are over 60 small bars, each one with a different theme. I know it looks a little sketchy, but it totally wasn't. There are definitely some more underground and alternative themes, so it took us a little trial and error to find one that matched our vibe. But we found a winner at Nice bar. Definitely recommend this spot, but also take the time to explore because there are a lot of gems here. Shinsekai is an old retro district that gives serious Coney island island vibes. Sutenkaku Tower is the famous backdrop and I recommend coming a little before sunset so you can see it. Less crowded, but definitely hang out at night too for the full vibe and neon signs all lit up, you'll find a ton of carnival games, old school arcades and lots of street food. This is where it said that Kushikatsu was invented, which are deep fried skewers of practically every type of protein and vegetable. I visited a bunch of markets while in Japan and Kuroman Market was actually my favorite. Specifically for this bluefin tuna spot, we lined up about 10 minutes before they opened and sat maybe 20 minutes later. The fish is super fresh and you choose your own cut and tell them how you want it prepared. We got medium fatty nigiri and when I tell you this was maybe the best tuna and nigiri I've ever had. My favorite shrine and temple I visited in Osaka are right in the city center. Nambayasaka Jinja was so unique and the moss covered statue at Hozenji Temple was very peaceful and beautiful. Of course you have to explore Dontonburi. I recommend coming both during the day and at night. We actually thought the Don Quixote Ferris wheel was a lot of fun and had a great view of the area. You of course got to stop by the famous Glitgo sign and I recommend a nighttime river cruise. We went with tonburi and learned a lot of interesting history. Of course, Dontonburi is known for street food like okay and okonomiyaki. I will admit I wasn't super impressed with the food here, so we specifically went to an okonomiyaki restaurant because it's one of my favorite dishes. We also got a yakisoba and both were so good. I definitely recommend this spot and bonus, it's quite close to Osaka Castle which we went to before getting lunch. We decided to just stick to the grounds and stuff going inside. But if you want to go inside, there's an admission fee and I believe it's basically a small museum. Of course, don't forget to save time to just wander and explore the area. You'll find so many opportunities to shop and eat. We kept stumbling on live music during the weekend and we loved walking around America Mura where there are a bunch of deck decorated light posts. If you're heading to Japan and looking for more things to do and places to eat in Tokyo and Kyoto, check out my highlight because I've already posted a bunch of recommendations.
